---
format:
  html:
    css: assets/css/custom.css
    page-layout: full
    toc: false
    title-block-banner: false
---


::: {.hero-shell}
::: {.hero-image role="img" aria-label="Placeholder for Everglades photograph"}
:::

::: {.hero-copy .container}
# Thinking Across Scales

How studying landscapes became a way of understanding the world.
:::
:::

::: {.section .intro-shell .container}
::: {.intro-copy .text-center .mx-auto}
I was trained as a landscape ecologist, where the most important lesson was not about landscapes themselves, but about learning to think across scales. Over time, that perspective expanded beyond ecology into computation, organizations, design, and everyday life. This website is an evolving record of that journey.
:::
:::

::: {.section .container-fluid .feature-cards-shell}
::: {.row .gx-4 .gy-4}
::: {.col-12 .col-md-6 .col-xl-3}
::: {.feature-card}
::: {.feature-icon}
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-scaling-icon lucide-scaling"><path d="M12 3H5a2 2 0 0 0-2 2v14a2 2 0 0 0 2 2h14a2 2 0 0 0 2-2v-7"/><path d="M14 15H9v-5"/><path d="M16 3h5v5"/><path d="M21 3 9 15"/></svg>
:::

### Thinking Across Scales

Ideas about feedback, hierarchy, dynamics, and systems.

[Explore →](ways-of-thinking/)
:::
:::

::: {.col-12 .col-md-6 .col-xl-3}
::: {.feature-card}
::: {.feature-icon}
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-map-plus-icon lucide-map-plus"><path d="m11 19-1.106-.552a2 2 0 0 0-1.788 0l-3.659 1.83A1 1 0 0 1 3 19.381V6.618a1 1 0 0 1 .553-.894l4.553-2.277a2 2 0 0 1 1.788 0l4.212 2.106a2 2 0 0 0 1.788 0l3.659-1.83A1 1 0 0 1 21 4.619V12"/><path d="M15 5.764V12"/><path d="M18 15v6"/><path d="M21 18h-6"/><path d="M9 3.236v15"/></svg>
:::

### Atlas

Places that transformed how I think.

[Explore →](journeys/)
:::
:::

::: {.col-12 .col-md-6 .col-xl-3}
::: {.feature-card}
::: {.feature-icon}
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-brain-circuit-icon lucide-brain-circuit"><path d="M12 5a3 3 0 1 0-5.997.125 4 4 0 0 0-2.526 5.77 4 4 0 0 0 .556 6.588A4 4 0 1 0 12 18Z"/><path d="M9 13a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 3-4"/><path d="M6.003 5.125A3 3 0 0 0 6.401 6.5"/><path d="M3.477 10.896a4 4 0 0 1 .585-.396"/><path d="M6 18a4 4 0 0 1-1.967-.516"/><path d="M12 13h4"/><path d="M12 18h6a2 2 0 0 1 2 2v1"/><path d="M12 8h8"/><path d="M16 8V5a2 2 0 0 1 2-2"/><circle cx="16" cy="13" r=".5"/><circle cx="18" cy="3" r=".5"/><circle cx="20" cy="21" r=".5"/><circle cx="20" cy="8" r=".5"/></svg>
:::

### Computational Thinking

Modern scientific workflows and digital craftsmanship.

[Explore →](#)
:::
:::

::: {.col-12 .col-md-6 .col-xl-3}
::: {.feature-card}
::: {.feature-icon}
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-notebook-pen-icon lucide-notebook-pen"><path d="M13.4 2H6a2 2 0 0 0-2 2v16a2 2 0 0 0 2 2h12a2 2 0 0 0 2-2v-7.4"/><path d="M2 6h4"/><path d="M2 10h4"/><path d="M2 14h4"/><path d="M2 18h4"/><path d="M21.378 5.626a1 1 0 1 0-3.004-3.004l-5.01 5.012a2 2 0 0 0-.506.854l-.837 2.87a.5.5 0 0 0 .62.62l2.87-.837a2 2 0 0 0 .854-.506z"/></svg>
:::

### Essays

Essays, notes, and ongoing reflections.

[Explore →](#)
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.section .container-fluid .highlight-shell}
::: {.row .gx-4 .gy-4}
::: {.col-12 .col-md-6}
::: {.highlight-panel}
::: {.panel-image}
:::

::: {.panel-copy}
Featured Essay

### Why Scale Matters

An exploration of scale as a guiding principle for science, design, and systems thinking.

[Read More →](#)
:::
:::
:::

::: {.col-12 .col-md-6}
::: {.highlight-panel}
::: {.panel-image}
:::

::: {.panel-copy}
Latest Journey

### Everglades

A field story about water, fire, networks, and the scales where landscape processes become visible.

[View Journey →](#)
:::
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.section .quote-shell}
> “I am not interested in whether two systems look alike. I am interested in whether they are organized by the same principles.”
:::



::: {.site-footer}

### Thinking Across Scales

Original field observations.  
Original photography.  
Original writing.

<br>

© 2026 Jing Yuan. All rights reserved.

Designed and written by Jing Yuan.  
Built with Quarto.

:::
